message:
chore(beads): close franken_numpy-k74v.3

19 numpy class types (broadcast, busdaycalendar, dtype, errstate, finfo,
flatiter, iinfo, int_, long, matrix, memmap, ndarray, ndenumerate,
ndindex, nditer, poly1d, recarray, record, ufunc) re-exported from
fnp_python via passthrough — commit 9db252a.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -477,7 +477,7 @@
 {"id":"franken_numpy-k74v","title":"[REALITY-CHECK-EPIC] Close fnp_python passthrough gap for numpy.__all__ (~200 wrappers)","description":"Parent epic for the ~200 missing passthrough wrappers that would raise fnp_python's numpy.__all__ coverage from 43% to ~100%.

Scope: per-category child beads (P0/P1/P2/P3) covering 70 core ufuncs, 106 core functions, 21 classes, ~15 constants/indexing/version items. Excludes dtype scalars (50 items, Category 4) since those can be re-exported wholesale via a single registration sweep.

Strategy: mirrors the established pattern in fnp-python/src/lib.rs (one #[pyfunction] delegating to numpy.<name>, registered in module init, listed in module_exports_python_surface smoke test, plus a dedicated <name>_matches_numpy_* parity oracle test). Each wrapper ~30-80 lines including tests.

Gating question (resolved by separate strategy-question bead): whether to structure as nested submodules (fnp_python.linalg.svd) or keep flat (linalg_svd).

Audit artifact: audit_numpy_reality.md.
Verification: cargo test -p fnp-python module_exports_python_surface must list every new name; full module_exports_python_surface still passes; new <name>_matches_numpy_* tests pass individually via rch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:20:03.423927977Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:20:03.423927977Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-k74v.1","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] Add 70 missing core numpy ufunc passthrough wrappers to fnp_python","description":"Parent: franken_numpy-k74v (REALITY-CHECK-EPIC).

Missing ufunc names (from numpy.__all__ intersect numpy.ufunc, not present in fnp_python):
  abs, absolute, acos, acosh, add, arccos, arccosh, arcsin, arcsinh, arctan, arctan2,
  arctanh, asin, asinh, atan, atan2, atanh, bitwise_and, bitwise_count, bitwise_invert,
  bitwise_left_shift, bitwise_not, bitwise_or, bitwise_right_shift, bitwise_xor, conj,
  cos, cosh, divide, divmod, equal, exp, exp2, float_power, fmax, fmin, gcd, greater,
  greater_equal, isnat, lcm, left_shift, less, less_equal, log, log10, log2,
  logical_and, logical_not, logical_or, logical_xor, matmul, maximum, minimum, mod,
  multiply, not_equal, power, remainder, right_shift, sin, sinh, sqrt, subtract,
  tan, tanh, matrix_transpose (dup), vecmat, matvec

Pattern: one-line passthrough wrapper like existing square/cbrt/expm1/fabs/negative.
Each follows:

  #[pyfunction]
  #[pyo3(signature = (x,))]       // or (x1, x2) for binary
  fn <name>(py: Python<'_>, x: Py<PyAny>) -> PyResult<Py<PyAny>> {
      let numpy = py.import("numpy")?;
      Ok(numpy.getattr("<name>")?.call1((x.bind(py),))?.unbind())
  }

Register in module init and module_exports_python_surface smoke test. Add one parity oracle test per family (trig, bitwise, comparison, etc.) rather than per-function to keep test count manageable.

Trig (12): acos, acosh, arccos, arccosh, arcsin, arcsinh, arctan, arctanh, asin, asinh, atan, atanh (note asin/asinh etc. are Array-API aliases for arcsin/etc.)
Inverse-trig-binary (2): arctan2, atan2
Hyperbolic (4): cosh, sinh, tanh, (asinh/acosh/atanh already counted)
Arithmetic (10): add, subtract, multiply, divide, true_divide (dup), power, float_power, mod, remainder, divmod
Comparison (6): equal, not_equal, greater, greater_equal, less, less_equal
Logical (4): logical_and, logical_not, logical_or, logical_xor
Bitwise (11): bitwise_and, bitwise_count, bitwise_invert, bitwise_left_shift, bitwise_not, bitwise_or, bitwise_right_shift, bitwise_xor, left_shift, right_shift, gcd, lcm
Exp/Log (6): exp, exp2, log, log10, log2, sqrt
Rounding/Elementary (4): abs, absolute, conj, isnat
Elementwise binary (4): fmax, fmin, maximum, minimum
Matrix ops (3): matmul, matvec, vecmat
Trig one-arg (3): sin, cos, tan

Priority: P0-P1. These are the numpy API baseline — every tutorial uses np.add, np.sin, np.log, np.sqrt.

Verification: rch exec -- cargo test -p fnp-python <family>_matches; cargo test -p fnp-python module_exports_python_surface.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:20:52.687278654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:20:52.687278654Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-k74v.1","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-k74v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:20:52.687278654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-k74v.2","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] Add ~100 missing core numpy function passthrough wrappers to fnp_python","description":"Parent: franken_numpy-k74v (REALITY-CHECK-EPIC).

Missing function names (from numpy.__all__ intersect plain functions, not present in fnp_python). Grouped by priority:

P0 (tutorial-critical):
  all, any, amax, amin, max, min, sum, prod, mean, median, std, var, argmax, argmin,
  array, empty, ones, zeros, astype, dot, einsum, sort, where, transpose (present),
  take, unique, cumsum, cumprod, cumulative_prod, cumulative_sum, around, trace

P1 (commonly used):
  apply_along_axis, apply_over_axes, atleast_1d, atleast_2d, atleast_3d, block,
  can_cast, concat, convolve, correlate, hsplit, interp, isclose, isdtype, isfortran,
  isnan, isneginf, issubdtype, matrix_transpose, moveaxis, nanargmax, nanargmin,
  nancumprod, nancumsum, nanmax, nanmin, nanpercentile, nanquantile, nanstd, nansum,
  nanvar, piecewise, place, printoptions, promote_types, put_along_axis, putmask,
  ravel (present? check), repeat, result_type, rollaxis (present), round, searchsorted,
  setbufsize, set_printoptions, shape, squeeze, swapaxes, take_along_axis, tensordot,
  trim_zeros, unique_all, unique_counts, unique_inverse, unique_values, unravel_index,
  vdot, ediff1d, histogram, histogram2d, histogramdd, tri, tril_indices, triu_indices,
  trapezoid, trapz

P2 (niche / legacy):
  array2string, array_repr, array_str, asmatrix, base_repr, binary_repr, bmat,
  busday_count, busday_offset, datetime_as_string, datetime_data, format_float_positional,
  format_float_scientific, get_array_wrap, get_include, is_busday, lookfor,
  maximum_sctype, meshgrid (present), printoptions, source

Notes:
- Some names here ARE already exposed under aliases (e.g. sum exists as size_count?? verify; argmax exists as ma_argmax but NOT as top-level argmax). Before starting each family, grep lib.rs for the exact name AND run: rg -w <name> crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs to determine actual status.
- Several names here already have wrappers: transpose, meshgrid, rollaxis, nonzero, median, copy, etc. — the true net-new count is lower than 100 but still sizeable.

Recommended batching: ship 5 per commit using the established rhythm. Group by family (reduction ops, creation ops, nan-aware variants, indexing helpers, histogram family, etc.).

Verification: cargo test -p fnp-python module_exports_python_surface; dedicated <name>_matches_numpy_* parity tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:06.609180833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:06.609180833Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-k74v.2","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-k74v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:06.609180833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"franken_numpy-k74v.3","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] Add 21 numpy class passthroughs to fnp_python (errstate, finfo, iinfo, memmap, ...)","description":"Parent: franken_numpy-k74v.

Missing numpy classes:
  broadcast, busdaycalendar, dtype, errstate, finfo, flatiter, iinfo, int_, long,
  matrix, memmap, ndarray, ndenumerate, ndindex, nditer, poly1d, recarray, record,
  ufunc, uint, ulong

Priority split:
- P1 (practical passthroughs — trivial re-export):
  errstate (context manager, common in numerical code)
  finfo, iinfo (introspect float/int limits)
  memmap (fnp-io has native support — can expose as fnp_python.memmap passthrough OR as a real pyclass wrapping fnp-io)
  poly1d, matrix (legacy but still in tutorials)

- P2 (design discussion required):
  dtype, ndarray, nditer, ufunc, recarray, record
  These would ideally be proper pyclass definitions backed by fnp_python types (not passthrough to numpy) — that's a significant architecture decision. For parity-oracle mode, a passthrough re-export (fnp_python.ndarray = numpy.ndarray) is acceptable.

- P3 (rare / deprecated):
  broadcast, busdaycalendar, flatiter, int_, long, ndenumerate, ndindex, uint, ulong

Verification: getattr smoke test + type-name parity asserts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:55.513533624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:55.513533624Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-k74v.3","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-k74v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:55.513533624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

  matrix, memmap, ndarray, ndenumerate, ndindex, nditer, poly1d, recarray, record,
  ufunc, uint, ulong

Priority split:
- P1 (practical passthroughs — trivial re-export):
  errstate (context manager, common in numerical code)
  finfo, iinfo (introspect float/int limits)
  memmap (fnp-io has native support — can expose as fnp_python.memmap passthrough OR as a real pyclass wrapping fnp-io)
  poly1d, matrix (legacy but still in tutorials)

- P2 (design discussion required):
  dtype, ndarray, nditer, ufunc, recarray, record
  These would ideally be proper pyclass definitions backed by fnp_python types (not passthrough to numpy) — that's a significant architecture decision. For parity-oracle mode, a passthrough re-export (fnp_python.ndarray = numpy.ndarray) is acceptable.

- P3 (rare / deprecated):
  broadcast, busdaycalendar, flatiter, int_, long, ndenumerate, ndindex, uint, ulong

Verification: getattr smoke test + type-name parity asserts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:55.513533624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:47:51.387442185Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:47:51.385611439Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — 19 numpy class names (broadcast, busdaycalendar, dtype, errstate, finfo, flatiter, iinfo, int_, long, matrix, memmap, ndarray, ndenumerate, ndindex, nditer, poly1d, recarray, record, ufunc) re-exported from fnp_python via numpy passthrough in commit 9db252a. Disjoint from k74v.4's dtype scalar set (uint, ulong live there, not here). Parity test module_reexports_numpy_class_types_matching_numpy asserts identity for 17 universal types + version-symmetric handling for int_/long. rch test: 5/5 passed. Future pyclass-native replacement (ndarray, dtype, ufunc) deferred to a separate architecture bead; passthrough is acceptable for parity-oracle mode per the bead spec.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-k74v.3","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-k74v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:55.513533624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-k74v.4","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] Re-export 50 numpy dtype scalar types from fnp_python","description":"Parent: franken_numpy-k74v.

Missing dtype scalar types:
  bool, bool_, byte, bytes_, cdouble, character, clongdouble, complex128, complex256,
  complex64, complexfloating, csingle, datetime64, double, flexible, float128, float16,
  float32, float64, floating, generic, half, inexact, int16, int32, int64, int8, intc,
  integer, intp, longdouble, longlong, number, object_, short, signedinteger, single,
  str_, timedelta64, ubyte, uint, uint16, uint32, uint64, uint8, uintc, uintp, ulong,
  ulonglong, ushort, unsignedinteger, void

These are NumPy scalar types, typically used as dtype= arguments. A single module-init sweep can re-export all of them:

  for name in ["int8", "int16", ...] { m.add(name, numpy.getattr(name)?)?; }

Implementation: ~15 lines of Rust in fnp_python(m) module init, iterating a const array of names.

Verification: assert_eq!(fnp_python.getattr("int8"), numpy.getattr("int8")) smoke tests for each; one parametric test is sufficient.

Priority P2: users can currently pass numpy.int8 directly to fnp_python wrappers (dtype: Py<PyAny>), so this is cosmetic but important for the 'full API' claim.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:09.470074161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:44:24.199950637Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:44:24.198099285Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — 52 numpy dtype scalar names (bool, bool_, byte, bytes_, cdouble, character, clongdouble, complex64/128/256, complexfloating, csingle, datetime64, double, flexible, float16/32/64/128, floating, generic, half, inexact, int8/16/32/64, intc, integer, intp, longdouble, longlong, number, object_, short, signedinteger, single, str_, timedelta64, ubyte, uint, uint8/16/32/64, uintc, uintp, ulong, ulonglong, ushort, unsignedinteger, void) re-exported from fnp_python via numpy passthrough in commit daebe80. Parity test module_reexports_numpy_dtype_scalar_types_matching_numpy asserts identity (ours.is(&theirs)) for 50 universal types and platform-symmetric handling for complex256/float128/bool. rch test: 4/4 passed. Platform-missing types skipped gracefully.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-k74v.4","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-k74v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:09.470074161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-k74v.5","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] Decide fnp_python submodule namespace strategy","description":"Parent: franken_numpy-k74v. Blocks per-category implementation beads.

Problem: fnp_python currently exposes numpy submodule content FLAT:
  fnp_python.linalg_svd       (vs numpy.linalg.svd)
  fnp_python.ma_argmax        (vs numpy.ma.argmax)
  fnp_python.testing_assert_allclose  (vs numpy.testing.assert_allclose)
  fnp_python.recfunctions_drop_fields (vs numpy.lib.recfunctions.drop_fields)

The README claims 'full API, edge cases and all'. Under flat namespace, a user doing:
  import fnp_python as np
  np.linalg.svd(...)
FAILS — must write np.linalg_svd(...).

Options:
  (A) Add real nested submodules via pyo3 PyModule::new(py, "linalg"). Requires restructuring lib.rs into submodule registration blocks plus duplicating wrappers (or re-registering them under nested modules). ~medium effort.
  (B) Keep flat; add fnp_python.linalg = types.SimpleNamespace(svd=fnp_python.linalg_svd, ...) in a Python-side __init__ or a module attribute constructed at init. Cheap but requires users to  and this depends on how the module is imported.
  (C) Keep flat and document in README that fnp_python uses flat namespace. Honest but breaks the 'drop-in' promise.

Recommendation: (A) is closest to vision; (B) is pragmatic; (C) is the honest pivot.

Decision required before implementation epic children can start. Currently blocks Category 5 (16 submodules) and also changes the shape of ufunc/function/class wrappers (should they register under fnp_python.<submodule>.<name>?).

Verification on resolution:
  python -c 'import fnp_python as np; np.linalg.svd(np.eye(3))' must succeed
  python -c 'import fnp_python.linalg' must succeed if real-submodule route chosen","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"question","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:23.641419177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:23.641419177Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-k74v.5","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-k74v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:23.641419177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-k74v.6","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] Expose numpy constants pi/e/nan/inf/euler_gamma/little_endian in fnp_python","description":"Parent: franken_numpy-k74v.

Missing numpy constants in fnp_python:
  pi, e, euler_gamma, inf, nan, little_endian

Implementation: ~6 lines of Rust in fnp_python(m) module init.

  use std::f64::consts::{PI, E};
  m.add("pi", PI)?;
  m.add("e", E)?;
  m.add("euler_gamma", 0.5772156649015329_f64)?;
  m.add("inf", f64::INFINITY)?;
  m.add("nan", f64::NAN)?;
  m.add("little_endian", cfg!(target_endian = "little"))?;

Verification: one test asserting fnp_python.<const> equals numpy.<const> element-wise. Note nan != nan so compare via numpy.isnan / bit-pattern.

Priority P2 — trivial. Skipping these is a minor but user-visible drawback.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:53.908926747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:21:33.846833890Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:21:33.844877808Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — 6 numpy top-level constants (pi, e, euler_gamma, inf, nan, little_endian) exposed in fnp_python pymodule init + parity test (module_exposes_numpy_top_level_constants_matching_numpy) verifying intrinsic values and agreement with numpy.<const>. Test: 1 passed. Commit 3727148.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-k74v.6","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-k74v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:53.908926747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
